1.gl: a day (as the warm hours), whether literal (from sunrise to sunset, or from one sunset to the next), or figurative (a space of time defined by an associated term), (often used adverb)
1.kj: age, [phrase] always, [phrase] chronicals, continually(-ance), daily, ((birth-), each, to) day, (now a, two) days (agone), [phrase] elder, [idiom] end, [phrase] evening, [phrase] (
1.dv: from an unused root meaning to be hot;

1.gl: to reign; inceptively, to ascend the throne; causatively, to induct into royalty; hence (by implication) to take counsel
1.kj: consult, [idiom] indeed, be (make, set a, set up) king, be (make) queen, (begin to, make to) reign(-ing), rule, [idiom] surely.

1.gl: to sate, i.e. fill to satisfaction (literally or figuratively)
1.kj: have enough, fill (full, self, with), be (to the) full (of), have plenty of, be satiate, satisfy (with), suffice, be weary of.

1.gl: Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also (typically) of his posterity
1.dv: from H8280 (שָׂרָה) and H410 (אֵל); he will rule as God;

1.gl: David, the youngest son of Jesse
1.dv: rarely (fully); דָּוִיד; from the same as H1730 (דּוֹד); loving;

2.gl: to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. remove (destroy, leave behind, put up, restore, etc.)

2.gl: literally one officiating, a priest; also (by courtesy) an acting priest (although a layman)
2.kj: chief ruler, [idiom] own, priest, prince, principal officer.

2.gl: a head person (of any rank or class)
2.kj: captain (that had rule), chief (captain), general, governor, keeper, lord,(-task-)master, prince(-ipal), ruler, steward.

2.gl: Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also (typically) of his posterity
2.dv: from H8280 (שָׂרָה) and H410 (אֵל); he will rule as God;
